title: SsrfSafeResolver on reqwest client — correct defense against DNS rebinding
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:1458-1480

FINDING: SsrfSafeResolver implements reqwest::dns::Resolve and is used as the DNS resolver for the shared http_client. This closes the DNS rebinding TOCTOU gap that exists when doing a pre-flight DNS check and then making the request separately — because the resolver used for the actual TCP connection is the same one performing the private-IP check, there is no window for a DNS rebinding attack to slip between check and connect.

The dual-layer defense (SsrfSafeResolver on the client + is_private_host_resolved pre-flight checks on actor/follower URIs) is deliberately redundant: the pre-flight checks provide early rejection with actionable error messages, while SsrfSafeResolver is the actual security guarantee. This is the correct architecture for defense-in-depth SSRF protection and should be preserved if someone proposes removing 'one of the two DNS checks'.
